sections.heading: Host Alerts
sections.body:
The conditions in this section apply if you select **Host** as
the alert target when configuring the alert. You can apply the
condition to all hosts or to specific type of host, such as
[primary](manual/reference/glossary) or
[config servers](manual/reference/glossary).

Atlas triggers certain host alerts based on cluster
monitoring, and are thus subject to variations in granularity. To
learn more, see [Monitoring Data Storage Granularity](monitoring-storage-granularity).

sections.heading: Asserts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the rate of asserts for a
MongoDB process, as collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus`
command's [asserts](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view asserts
through cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Auto-Scaling
sections.body:
You can configure alerts for the following cluster events.
[Project Activity Feed](view-activity-feed) to review all
auto-scaling events that occurred.

For each event in this section, to receive alerts, you must first configure
an alert to notify you or members of your organization of this type of
an auto-scaling event.

To learn how Atlas scales a cluster up or down, see [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling).

sections.heading: Write-Blocking
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to [write-blocking behavior](cluster-blocking-writes)
in Atlas.

sections.heading: MongoDB Search
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the amount of CPU and memory
used by MongoDB Search processes. You can view MongoDB Search metrics through
[MongoDB Search charts](review-atlas-search-metrics).

sections.heading: Average Execution Time
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the average execution time of
reads, writes, or commands for a MongoDB process, as collected from the
MongoDB `serverStatus` command's [opLatencies](manual/reference/command/serverStatus)
document. You can view asserts through
cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Disk Throughput
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the disk read and write
throughput for a MongoDB process. You
can view these metrics on the Atlas **Disk Throughput**
chart, accessed through [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics).

sections.heading: Opcounter
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the rate of database operations
on a MongoDB process since the process last started, as collected from
the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's [opcounters](manual/reference/command/serverStatus)
document. You can view opcounters through
cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Opcounter - Repl
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the rate of database operations
on MongoDB [secondary](manual/reference/glossary), as collected from the
MongoDB `serverStatus` command's [opcountersRepl](manual/reference/command/serverStatus)
document. You can view these metrics on the
**Opcounters - Repl** chart, accessed through
cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Operations Scan and Order
sections.body:
You might set alerts for the scan and order operations for a MongoDB
process.

sections.heading: Memory
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure memory for a MongoDB process, as
collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[mem](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view these metrics on the
Atlas **Memory** and **Non-Mapped Virtual Memory**
charts, accessed through cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Connections
sections.body:
The following alert condition measures connections to a MongoDB
process, as collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[connections](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view this metric on the
Atlas **Connections** chart, accessed through cluster
monitoring.

sections.heading: Queues
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure operations waiting on locks, as
collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[globalLock](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view these metrics on the
Atlas **Queues** chart, accessed through cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Page Faults
sections.body:
The following alert condition measures the rate of page faults for a
MongoDB process, as collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus`
command's [extra\_info.page\_faults](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) field.

sections.heading: Cursors
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure the number of
[cursors](manual/core/cursors) for a MongoDB process, as collected
from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[metrics.cursor](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view these metrics on
the Atlas **Cursors** chart, accessed through
cluster monitoring.

sections.heading: Network
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure throughput for MongoDB process,
as collected from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[network](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) document. You can view these metrics on a
host's **Network** chart, accessed through cluster
monitoring.

sections.heading: Replication Oplog
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to the MongoDB process's
[oplog](manual/core/replica-set-oplog). You can view these metrics
on the following charts, accessed through
cluster monitoring:

- **Oplog GB/Hour**

- **Replication Headroom**

- **Replication Lag**

- **Replication Oplog Window**

The following alert conditions apply to the oplog:

sections.heading: DB Storage
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to database storage, as collected
for a MongoDB process by the MongoDB
[dbStats](manual/reference/command/dbStats) command. For details on
how Atlas handles reaching database storage limits, refer to the
[FAQ page](<faq-storage limit>). These conditions are based on the
summed total of **all databases** on the MongoDB process:

> **Note:** 
>
> Atlas retrieves database metrics every 20 minutes by default but
> adjusts frequency when necessary to reduce the impact on database
> performance.

sections.heading: Namespaces
sections.body:
The following alert condition applies to the total number of namespaces
across all non-system databases in a MongoDB process. You can view how
many namespaces are in use for a MongoDB process by viewing the
the **Catalog** [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics).

sections.heading: WiredTiger Storage Engine
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to the MongoDB process's
[WiredTiger](manual/core/wiredtiger) storage engine, as collected
from the MongoDB `serverStatus` command's
[wiredTiger.cache](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) and
[queues.execution](manual/reference/command/serverStatus) documents.

You can view these metrics on the following charts, accessed through
cluster monitoring:

- **Cache Activity**

- **Cache Usage**

- **Tickets Available**

- **Cache Fill Ratio**

The following are the alert conditions that apply to WiredTiger:

For clusters running on MongoDB version 7.0
and later, don't use the number of tickets as a metric for
overload alerts. Starting in MongoDB version 7.0, Atlas
dynamically adjusts the number of tickets. Instead, use the
number of queued readers and writers as an overload metric.

sections.heading: System and Disk Alerts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions measure usage on your Atlas server
clusters:

> **Note:** Currently, Atlas uses a single partition for data, index, and
> journal files. Even though the alerts reference individual
> partitions, they point to the same metric.

> **Note:** All hardware metrics have burst reporting equivalents with distinct
> configurable alerts. To learn more, see [Burst Reporting](burst-metrics).

sections.heading: Inapplicable Host Conditions
sections.body:
The following host conditions do not apply to Atlas. Atlas will
not generate alerts for the following conditions:

- Accesses Not In Memory: Total is

- Background Flush Average is

- B-tree: accesses is

- B-tree: hits is

- B-tree: misses is

- B-tree: miss ratio is

- Cursors: Client Cursors Size is

- Effective Lock % is

- Journaling Commits in Write Lock is

- Journaling MB is

- Journaling Write Data Files MB is

- Memory: Mapped is

- Page Fault Exceptions Thrown: Total is

sections.heading: Query Targeting Alerts
sections.body:
The following alerts apply to indexes on your collections. Either alert
might indicate a missing or inefficient index.

> **See Also:** To learn more about indexing to improve performance, see
> [Indexing Strategies](manual/applications/indexes).

The [change streams](manual/changeStreams) cursors that the MongoDB Search
process (`mongot`) uses to keep MongoDB Search indexes updated can
contribute to the query targeting ratio and trigger
[query targeting alerts](query-targeting-alerts) if the ratio
is high.

sections.heading: Cloud Backup Alerts
sections.body: The following alerts apply to Cloud Backup snapshots.

sections.heading: User Alerts
sections.body: The following alert conditions apply to Atlas users.

sections.heading: Project Alerts
sections.body: The following alert conditions apply to your Atlas project.

sections.heading: Billing Alerts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to Atlas billing. You can
configure billing alerts from the Atlas UI at the organization
level or the project level.

To configure organization-level alerts:

sections.heading: Step 1: Configure the alerts.
sections.body: To configure project-level alerts:

sections.heading: Step 1: Configure the alerts.
sections.body: > **Note:** All amounts billed are in USD (United States Dollars).

sections.heading: Service Account Alerts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to Atlas [Service Account](service-accounts-overview). You can
[Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) from the
Atlas UI at the organization level.

sections.heading: Encryption at Rest Alerts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to projects using

sections.heading: Maintenance Window Alerts
sections.body:
The following alert conditions apply to projects with
[maintenance](atlas-modify-project-settings).

> **Note:** You can only [Configure a Maintenance Window Alert](configure-mw-alert) if a project has an active maintenance window.

sections.heading: Atlas Stream Processing Alerts
sections.body: The following alert conditions apply to projects running Stream Processing Workspaces.
